tagline: Senior strategic marketing support for food & drink SMEs with real ambitions to grow.

sections.title: Build the foundations that turn good products into *growing brands*
sections.sub: Senior strategic marketing support for food and drink SMEs with real ambitions to grow — but not enough in-house marketing expertise to make it happen.
sections.sub2: If you know something needs to change, I help you work out where to focus, what your brand should stand for, and how to turn both into action.

sections.body:
Many food and drink businesses spend most of their time on promotional tactics, without regularly stepping back — promoting what they have, launching the next product, running the next campaign.

The result is activity that feels busy but can mean inconsistent messaging, missed opportunities, and the nagging feeling that something isn't quite right.

**Building the right foundations starts with understanding your customers, what makes your brand stand apart, and developing clear, focused strategies to guide the way.**

sections.title: Three ways to *work together*
sections.lead: Wherever you are, there's a clear next step — from a one-off strategic reset to ongoing senior support.

cards.title: Strategic Growth Roadmap
cards.body: Know you've got potential to grow but not sure where to start? A structured two-workshop process that gives you a clear, written growth roadmap.

cards.title: Brand Positioning
cards.body: Want clarity on who your brand is really for and how to talk about it consistently? We use a proven Brand House framework to define it together.

cards.title: Ongoing Strategic Adviser
cards.body: Need someone to keep strategy on track? I work as your strategic adviser on a flexible retainer, making sure the right people do the right things.

sections.heading: I'm Nadia Henrique-Murray, founder of *Appétit*
sections.body:
With 25+ years working on nationally and internationally recognised food and drink brands — including Doom Bar, Jordan's, Campbell's, Homepride, Silver Spoon and Rodda's — I bring the strategic rigour of big-brand marketing to food and drink SMEs who want to get their marketing right.

I also have a proven track record with smaller businesses, helping founders make the most of leaner budgets and teams to get real results.

stats.label: Years on UK food & drink brands

sections.title: Strategic support, *shaped around you*
sections.lead: Three distinct ways to work together. Each starts with where you are and what would make the biggest difference, fastest.

sections.title: Strategic Growth Roadmap
sections.need: You know your business has potential to grow, but you're not sure where to start. You might sense what's not working, but lack the strategic clarity — or stakeholder alignment — to know what to tackle first.
sections.solution: A structured two-workshop process that gives you a clear, written Strategic Growth Roadmap, identifying your biggest brand and product growth priorities and the key strategies to address them.
items: Audit questionnaire ahead of the workshops
Workshop 1 — strengths, development needs, opportunities and threats, then your biggest growth opportunities
Workshop 2 — marketing objectives aligned to business goals, and strategies to meet them
Final output — a written Strategic Growth Roadmap focused on the right priorities

sections.title: Brand Positioning
sections.need: You want clarity on who your brand is really for, what makes it stand apart, and how to talk about it consistently — whether you're starting from scratch, rebranding, or realising different people say different things.
sections.solution: Using a proven Brand House framework, I work with you and your team to develop a well-defined target market and brand positioning, covering every essential building block.
items: Best as a follow-on from the Growth Roadmap, but works standalone
Collaborative workshops with all key stakeholders together
Market definition, competitors, root strengths, key messages, personality and values
A written brand positioning document to guide all plans and decisions

sections.title: Ongoing Strategic Adviser
sections.need: Once clear on priorities, you need someone to help translate them into action. You may already work with junior marketers, freelancers or agencies — but they need clear strategic direction and briefs you don't have time to provide.
sections.solution: I work as your strategic marketing adviser on a flexible retainer, providing the direction and oversight to make sure the right people are doing the right things, properly briefed, and producing work that's fit for purpose.
items: Flexible retainer — minimum 1 day / month over 6 months
Strategic guidance for brand and marketing plans aligned to your goals
Reviewing work before it goes live

sections.title: Not sure which is right for you?
sections.body: Start with a free, no-obligation discovery call. We'll work out where you are and whether I can help.

sections.heading: Big-brand rigour, *small-business pace*
sections.body:
The 25 years I spent in marketing roles across some of the UK's best-known food and drink brands were hugely formative — brands at very different points in their journeys, across a wide variety of categories and audiences.

My experience spanned both brand and product management, giving me a deep understanding of how to grow product portfolios through improvements, new product development, pricing, category management, and the very different dynamics of channels from corner shops to major multiples, wholesale and hospitality.

Much of that experience was in FMCG — widely recognised as one of the best training grounds for rigorous, consumer-led and commercially demanding marketing. It also meant a lot of time in large organisations, where corporate politics could get in the way of the work that mattered, where moving fast was difficult, and where it wasn't always possible to work on things I truly believed in.

**Making the jump to working independently has felt genuinely liberating.**

What I discovered is how much faster you can make a real, positive difference with smaller businesses. There's less distance between a good idea and it actually happening — which I find energising.

I'm originally from France — a country renowned for its love of good food and drink — and equally influenced by my Italian and Portuguese heritage. I came to the UK as a student and built my entire career here.

sections.title: Food & drink brands *I've worked on*
sections.lead: Across employed and independent roles — from household names to challenger brands.
sections.note: Brand names shown for reference — replace with supplied logo artwork.

items.title: Diagnosis before prescription
items.body: I ask the right questions before offering answers, and work from what you've built rather than assuming everything needs to change.

items.title: Partner, not just adviser
items.body: I work collaboratively with you and your team — I don't just deliver a strategy and leave you to figure out how to turn it into action.

items.title: Plain-speaking
items.body: Clear, jargon-free guidance focused on what will actually make a difference.

items.title: Commercially focused
items.body: Every recommendation is connected to business outcomes. Marketing exists to drive the business, not just look good.

items.title: Invested in your success
items.body: Motivated by the difference good marketing makes to your business — not by billing more days than are needed.

sections.title: Ready to get your marketing on the right track?
sections.body: The best place to start is a conversation. Get in touch to arrange a free, no-obligation discovery call.

sections.title: Let's have a *conversation*
sections.lead: The first step is always a free, no-obligation discovery call — a chance to talk about where you are, what you're trying to achieve, and whether I can help. No jargon, no hard sell.
sections.successBody: I'll be in touch within a couple of working days to arrange your discovery call.

sections.title: A clear path from *where you are* to where you want to be
sections.sub: This whole page was assembled from reusable section blocks — edit it, reorder it, or delete it entirely in the Studio under Pages.

items.title: 1 · Discover
items.body: We start with a free discovery call to understand where you are and what you're trying to achieve.

items.title: 2 · Define
items.body: Through structured workshops we set priorities, positioning and a clear written plan.

items.title: 3 · Deliver
items.body: I help you turn the plan into action — briefing the right people and keeping strategy on track.

sections.heading: Foundations first, *then* the fun stuff
sections.body:
Most marketing problems aren't really marketing problems — they're clarity problems. Get the foundations right and everything downstream gets easier.

That's why every engagement starts with understanding your customers and what makes you different.

sections.title: Curious whether this would work for you?
sections.body: Book a free, no-obligation discovery call and let's find out together.
